title: Best Restaurants in Rayong Thailand (2026)
meta_description: The Rayong restaurants that actually help plan the trip: Ban Phe seafood logic, a real old-town food walk, one reliable city-center dinner, and one edited coast or island splurge.
intro: Rayong food gets weaker when every restaurant is chosen by scraped ratings and anonymous review counts. The better shortlist keeps Rayong's actual dining patterns visible: Ban Phe seafood, the Yomjinda old-town evening layer, one city-center hotel dinner that is genuinely useful, and one coastal or island meal that is worth planning on purpose.

items.name: Yomjinda Road food walk
items.description: The best non-resort evening food move in Rayong because the old-town street, snack stops and local rhythm all reinforce one another.
insider_tips: Walk first and eat in stages instead of treating Yomjinda like a one-table dinner reservation.
items.current_info: Novotel Rayong Star's local guide still frames Yomjinda through old-town buildings, local food stalls, and night-market life, which is exactly why it deserves a slot over generic rankings.

items.name: Laem Charoen Seafood
items.description: A durable Rayong seafood pick because the official branch list still keeps Rayong in the network and the name carries more local food weight than copy-pasted "best seafood" filler.
highlights: Good dependable lunch or dinner anchor
insider_tips: Use this when you want one stable seafood stop without guessing from review platforms.
items.current_info: A stronger official-name seafood answer than another scraped list of near-identical waterfront restaurants.

items.name: The Brasserie
items.description: The useful city-center restaurant when you want a reliable breakfast or a planned buffet dinner on a Rayong town stay.
items.location: Holiday Inn & Suites Rayong City Centre
insider_tips: Best on arrival or departure nights when you want a clean in-city dinner without another transfer.
items.current_info: Holiday Inn's official dining page positions The Brasserie as the property's global-cuisine restaurant with breakfast and a midweek buffet dinner.

items.name: Elementi Rooftop Restaurant & Bar
items.description: The sharper city splurge when the point is rooftop timing and a more deliberate dinner than Rayong's casual local-food stops.
items.location: Holiday Inn & Suites Rayong City Centre
insider_tips: Keep this for one deliberate evening rather than making every city night a rooftop dinner.
items.current_info: Holiday Inn Rayong's official materials continue to use Elementi as the property's rooftop restaurant and bar, which gives it a clearer planning role than generic city-steak filler.

items.name: Fish Bar
items.description: The better mainland-resort seafood dinner if you want a coastal setting without turning the stay into a Koh Samet booking.
insider_tips: Best when you are already staying on the Klaeng or Wang Kaew coast rather than driving purely for dinner.
items.current_info: Marriott's official dining page still positions Fish Bar as the on-property seafood restaurant with grilled-to-order seafood.

items.name: Ao Prao Resort Restaurant
items.description: The best island-side dinner choice when you are staying on the quieter west coast of Koh Samet and want the sunset setting to be part of the meal.
highlights: Thai, international, seafood and BBQ menu
insider_tips: Use this only if you are actually staying on Ao Prao or planning a proper island extension.
items.current_info: Ao Prao Resort's official restaurant page describes a beachfront restaurant on Ao Prao Beach with Thai, international, seafood and BBQ dishes from morning through late evening.
